zotero.abstractNote: Boosting Variational Inference (BVI) is a popular framework for approximating complex posterior distributions by iteratively adding components to a mixture model. A key challenge in BVI is the selection of the step-size at each iteration. Existing methods either use a fixed step-size, which can be slow to converge, or perform a line search, which can be computationally expensive. In this paper, we propose a novel approach to BVI that uses locally adaptive step-sizes. Our method is based on the observation that the optimal step-size can be estimated by considering the local geometry of the objective function. We derive a closed-form update rule for the step-size that is computationally efficient and does not require line search. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach on a variety of Bayesian inference tasks, showing that it consistently outperforms existing BVI methods in terms of convergence speed and final approximation quality.
zotero.conferenceName: International Joint Conference on Artificial Intelligence
zotero.proceedingsTitle: Proceedings of the 30th International Joint Conference on Artificial Intelligence (IJCAI)
